status.info: We use this protocol and it's working

steps.step: {"blocks":[{"key":"eo1tp","text":"Collect the cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF)    (64-77°F / 18-25°C). No fasting is required. Once collected, keep samples at   or at  .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":39,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":118,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":126,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"0EEE78A0AE5711EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"4","unit":32}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"09D34030AE5711EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"4","unit":165}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"078335B0AE5711EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"4","unit":10}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"f4f62","text":"Perform lumbar puncture (LP) utilizing Sprotte atraumatic lumbar needle with cannula.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dn2hr","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7hd3l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"58qc6","text":"In case of bleeding at the puncture site, collect the first 1-2 mL of CSF fluid in the CSF WASTE tube.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"2p85i","text":"Continue collecting CSF fluid in the CSF WASTE tube until CSF is visibly devoid of red blood cells (RBC) contamination, i.e., no pinkish or red color. For the CSF sample, collect   of CSF fluid into pre-labeled tubes. Proceed with the next steps even if less than   of CSF was collected. Replace caps after CSF sample collection and mix gently by inverting each tube 3-4 times. CSF WASTE tube may be discarded.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":179,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":264,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"30","error":false,"guid":"06C8C9E0AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"30","error":false,"guid":"0D8AD480AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"cqq7q","text":"Record the total CSF volume and if CSF sample looks grossly or visibly contaminated with RBC and/or if it is hued pink or red. Record the date and time of the CSF sample collected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4cnal","text":"Transfer the CSF sample as quickly as possible to the lab. Then proceed to CSF centrifugation and processing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"3s61e","text":"Spin down the CSF cells at   using a swing bucket centrifuge.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":27,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a7s69","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"centrifuge","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"centrifuge":"300","duration":600,"error_centrifuge":false,"error_temperature":false,"guid":"3B10BAF0AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"scientificNotation":{"centrifuge":false,"temperature":false},"start":null,"temperature":"4","temperatureUnit":10,"unit":34}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"21b41","text":"Aspirate the supernatant with a pipette, leaving ~  without disturbing the cell pellet. Save the CSF supernatant   for further aliquots.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":113,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4p36c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"500","error":false,"guid":"4DFC7AF0AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"549E5360AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"4","unit":165}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"1nnes","text":"Mix and count the total CSF cell number. Use   of the sample with 1:1 dilution of trypan blue and a C-Chip disposable hemacytometer (DHCN015). Record the total CSF cell number.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":45,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"5","error":false,"guid":"5D3B3880AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"8aemi","text":"Transfer to a 1.5-mL Eppendorf tube and spin down at   using a swing bucket centrifuge.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":53,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2g8h1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"centrifuge","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"centrifuge":"300","duration":600,"error_centrifuge":false,"error_temperature":false,"guid":"68D61B60AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"scientificNotation":{"centrifuge":false,"temperature":false},"start":null,"temperature":"4","temperatureUnit":10,"unit":34}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"d3no0","text":"Carefully aspirate the supernatant, leaving ~ .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":45,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"bnbj8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"50","error":false,"guid":"82D428E0AE5811EC8C899B4564E1158A","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}}}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"3brl3","text":"If the cells need to be diluted more, use the CSF supernatant. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"bnjam","text":"If there are leftover CSF cells after 10x loading, freeze down the cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"eog1t","text":"Submit for 10x single cell loading. Ideally 700-1200 cells/ul.
